_meta.title: Wire C# entry points through to the CoCoR grammar callbacks
_meta.rationale: Closes the cross-language gap from C# (ToolingServer.cs / TdmToolData.cs InitClrTmsInterface) down through the C++/CLI bridge (TmsNCLink) into the unmanaged interpreter (tlsSoc -> tlsSocP -> tlsbSocP -> cocInterpreter). InitClrTmsInterface itself only constructs TmsNCLink + Init; the actual script-execution path is InitClrTmsInterface (lifecycle) + ExecuteScriptThread -> TmsNCLink::CallScript -> tlsSoc::Exec -> tlsSocP::Exec, which then drives the CocoR-generated Parser's gen->On* callbacks. The clrTdmScript.cpp file is a SEPARATE managed wrapper that reaches tlsSocP directly via OnDeclare — recorded as a parallel edge, not a sequel.

edges_to_add.note: new TmsNCLink() + tmsnclink.Init(sIniFileName, 2) — bootstraps the unmanaged TDM workspace
new TmsNCLink() + tmsnclink.Init(sIniFileName, 4) — bootstraps the unmanaged TDM workspace
Calls tmsnclink.CallScript(sScrName, alParams, ref sResult) — the runtime entry into the CoCoR pipeline
Calls tmsnclink.CallScript(sScrName, alParams, ref sResult) — the runtime entry into the CoCoR pipeline
TmsNCLink::CallScript constructs `tlsSoc pSoc(...)` on the stack to host one script invocation
pSoc.Exec(vsParaVal, sReturnValue, bStringType) — runs the script and reads `_Result` back
tlsSoc::Exec dispatches via m_pinterpreter — when type==tlsInterpreterTypeSocP, calls ((tlsSocP*)m_pinterpreter)->Exec (tlsSoc.cpp:466)
tlsSoc constructor factory-creates m_pinterpreter = new tlsSocP(sId, sProgram, ...) (tlsSoc.cpp:263)
tlsSocP::Exec inherits from cocInterpreter — invokes Parse() (which runs the CoCoR grammar) then Interpret() (which runs the bytecode VM, firing On* callbacks)

clrTdmScript.cpp:517 calls ((tlsSocP*)pSoc.GetInterpreter())->OnDeclare(...) directly to inject clrScriptObjects from .NET — a PARALLEL managed entry, separate from the TmsNCLink path. (OnDeclare not yet extracted as its own node; file-level edge until next /understand re-run)
